sections.paragraph:
ἀέναος, ον (in var. spellings [mostly w. one ν; on the etym. s. Boisacq.] Hes., Hdt.+; ins, pap, LXX. Adv. ἀεννάως ApcEsdr p. 33, 3 Tdf.).

① lit. ever-flowing of springs (Hes., Opera 595; Simonides Fgm. 48 Diehl [581, 2 Page] in Diog. L. 1, 90; Epict., Gnomol. 2 [p. 463 Sch.] fr. Stob.; M. Ant. 8, 51; Wsd 11:6; Philo, Spec. Leg. 1, 303; Jos., Bell. 3, 45, Ant. 3, 258; SibOr 4, 15) 1 Cl 20:10.

② fig. eternal (Pind. et al.; UPZ 14, 33 [II b.c.]; LXX; SibOr 3, 698; Herm. Wr. 18, 14a; PGM 13, 842) God (Sb 8141, 22 [ins I b.c.] θεοῦ μεγάλου ἔκγονος ἀενάου) PtK 2. τὴν ἀ. τοῦ κόσμου σύστασιν the everlasting constitution of the universe 1 Cl 60:1 (cp. OGI 56, 48 εἰς τὸν ἀ. κόσμον).—DELG s.v. αἰών.

var. var. = various(ly)

etym. etym. = etymology, -ical

Boisacq Boisacq = ÉB., Dictionnaire étymologique de la Langue Greque—List 6

Hes Hes , date uncertain, perh. before VI b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

ApcEsdr ApcEsdr = Apocalypse of Esdras—List 2

Tdf. Tdf. = CvTischendorf—List 1, beg.

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Diehl Diehl , ed., s. AnthLG—List 5

Page Page = DP., s. AnthLG—List 5

Diog. L. Diog. L. = Diogenes Laertius, III a.d.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Sch. Sch. = TSchermann, Prophetarum vitae fabulosae—List 5

  1. fr. = from

Stob Stob , V a.d.—List 5

  1. Ant. M. Ant. = Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, II a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Pind Pind , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

UPZ UPZ = Urkunden der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

PtK PtK = Petruskerygma (Preaching of Peter)—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 22
